economic_notes: KZA owns 100% of 3183092 Canada Inc. (Pasahigan Home Center, holding company), 4106679 Canada Ltd. (holding company owning 100% of Mitigog Forestry Enterprises Inc. which harvests timber for sale to local mills). Land claims global settlement of $116,801,811 ratified January 2019; $78,000,000 deposited in Kitigan Zibi Anishinabeg Trust (not consolidated). COVID-19 pandemic response measures enacted. 69% of revenue from Indigenous Services Canada and other federal agencies.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Nation is located in Quebec. Three government business enterprises accounted for under modified equity method: 3183092 Canada Inc. (Pasahigan Home Center), 4106679 Canada Ltd., and Mitigog Forestry Enterprises Inc. The Kitigan Zibi Anishinabeg Trust ($78M from land claims settlement) is NOT consolidated in these statements. Health Canada revenue of $4,451,285 appears in comparative 2020 column only — not in 2021 actuals on the face of the statement; total_government_transfers calculated from identified government revenue lines (ISC $26,592,188 + Health Canada $4,451,285 shown in 2020 comparative, FNEC $1,258,809, Solicitor General $925,568, Ministère des Finances QC $589,569, AFNQL $702,029, Ministère de la Famille $539,710, CMHC $78,488 = for 2021 excluding Health Canada: ISC $26,592,188 + FNEC $1,258,809 + Solicitor General $925,568 + MFQ $589,569 + AFNQL $702,029 + Famille $539,710 + CMHC $78,488 = $30,686,361; Health Canada line shows $4,451,285 only in 2020 column so excluded from 2021 total; total_government_transfers set to sum of identifiable 2021 government lines. COVID-19 schedule M expenses $263,261 included. Prior auditor issued unqualified opinion on 2020 statements March 12, 2021; KPMG audited 2021 statements signed June 16, 2022.
